It's crucial that we do our homework to identify what legitimate contrasts are genuinely present and available so that we can bring them to the attention of our prospects. To summarize, there are three things sleuths do to use the contrast phenomenon. First, they know contrast is always available. As my colleague Gregory Nidart says, if you can't find contrast, you're not looking hard enough. Second, they don't save the best for last. They lead with the biggest and best comparison. And third, they answer the question for their prospect, compared to what? So take the time to do your homework and see what meaningful contrast you can bring to the surface of your prospect's minds.
